markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Paris be 20°C or higher on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Nikema Williams commands 97.8% trader consensus to win Georgia's 5th Congressional District Democratic primary on May 19, driven by her fundraising dominance and incumbency advantages in the overwhelmingly Democratic Atlanta-based district. An April 24 Atlanta Journal-Constitution analysis underscored her strong re-election positioning against low-traction challengers Victor Hill—a former Clayton County sheriff with prior legal issues—Arnetress Beatty, and Andres Castro, amid no public polling showing competition. Recent endorsements, including from Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg, and Williams' community project funding wins reinforce her lead, aligning with historical high incumbency success rates in safe primaries. Late-breaking scandals, major challenger endorsements, or atypical voter turnout could theoretically challenge this, but significant barriers persist.

markets.question: Aston Villa FC vs. Tottenham Hotspur FC: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game between Aston Villa FC and Tottenham Hotspur FC, scheduled for May 3 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Aston Villa FC and Tottenham Hotspur FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Aston Villa FC vs. Tottenham Hotspur FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for May 3 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aston Villa host Tottenham Hotspur at Villa Park on May 3 in a rescheduled Premier League fixture, shifted due to Villa's Europa League semifinal advancement after a 7-1 aggregate win over Bologna. Sitting fourth with 54 points, Villa chase Champions League qualification amid strong recent form, bolstered by a lighter injury list despite concerns for Jadon Sancho (shoulder) and Emiliano Martinez (calf), with Boubacar Kamara out long-term. Tottenham, mired in relegation peril after a Sunderland defeat, face a defensive crisis without Cristian Romero (knee, season-ending), Guglielmo Vicario (groin), Mohammed Kudus (hamstring), and others like James Maddison (doubtful); Rodrigo Bentancur may return. Villa hold a recent head-to-head edge (3 wins in last 5), with home form and Spurs' injury woes shaping trader consensus on a competitive but Villa-favored matchup.

markets.question: US takes a stake in Spirit Airlines by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. federal government takes a stake in Spirit Airlines, or any of its parent or subsidiary companies, by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Takes a stake refers to the U.S. federal government acquiring direct equity ownership, voting shares, convertible rights treated as equity, or equivalent ownership interests in the listed company or of a legal vehicle that primarily owns the listed company. Stakes acquired through independent entities entirely controlled or owned by the U.S. federal government (e.g. a sovereign wealth fund, state-owned enterprise, etc.) will count. Non-equity financial instruments or stakes acquired by private persons or entities not owned or controlled by the US federal government will not count; acquisitions by states, pensions, index or mutual funds, or consortia will not qualify.

An official US federal government announcement of a completed qualifying acquisition, or of a binding agreement to complete a qualifying acquisition, within this market’s timeframe will be sufficient to resolve this market to “Yes”. Speculation, suggestions, plans, or other announcements which do not announce a completed acquisition or a binding acquisition agreement, however, will not count.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the US federal government and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Airlines' orderly wind-down of operations announced May 2, following the collapse of bailout negotiations with the Trump administration, drives overwhelming trader consensus at 95% against a US federal stake by May 31. Late-April talks for a $500 million infusion in exchange for up to 90% equity stake faltered amid bondholder rejections, unresolved bankruptcy proceedings, and surging fuel costs from geopolitical tensions, eliminating the carrier's viability without intervention. With flights ceased and liquidation underway, structural barriers like congressional appropriations hurdles and no active airline entity preclude action. Only an extraordinary executive order or emergency funding could shift odds, though precedent favors non-involvement post-failure.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Wuhan be 25°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/wuhan/ZHHH.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest ensemble forecasts from meteorological models, including those aggregated by AccuWeather, project Wuhan's highest temperature on May 5 at around 31°C (88°F), with increasing cloudiness and minimal precipitation risk under southerly winds at 13 km/h. This marks a warming trend from recent cooler conditions—observed highs near 21°C on May 2 amid a passing cool air mass—aligning with May's climatological average of 26–28°C as subtropical highs strengthen over central China. Historical data shows early May highs typically range 24–29°C, with low variability; however, short-term forecast uncertainty persists due to potential convective development or model shifts, with China Meteorological Administration updates expected daily through resolution. Traders monitor southerly flow and UV index for final positioning.

markets.question: Will there be no head of state of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Nicolás Maduro at 64% to remain Venezuela's recognized head of state by December 31, 2026, despite his January 3 capture by U.S. forces and ongoing detention in New York on drug and weapons charges, as the Maduro-aligned Supreme Court and certain state institutions continue to affirm his constitutional presidency, blocking a full transition. Delcy Rodríguez, his former vice president sworn in as interim leader, trades at 21% amid U.S. sanctions relief on April 1 and her extension beyond the 90-day limit without elections, reflecting de facto control but internal Chavista divisions and repression. Opposition leader María Corina Machado at 8% eyes a return and swift vote, though no timeline exists, underscoring uncertainty in the post-ouster stalemate.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

The Democratic Party outcome trades at 18%, aligning with historical underperformance despite a crowded primary field including Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief. Near-even odds on candidates A (likely Wilson), B (a GOP challenger such as Sam Gibbons or Hamp Redmond), and Other reflect pre-primary uncertainty ahead of the June 9 Republican and Democratic primaries, with no public polling yet available to shift sentiment.

Nathan MacKinnon's elite two-way impact—127 points atop superior 5-on-5 metrics (+58 goals-for/against differential)—earns 20% as Colorado's driving force, while Nikita Kucherov's 130 points amid Tampa Bay's injury challenges secure 19.3%. Macklin Celebrini's rookie surge fueled San Jose's 16-win improvement to 5.1%, but Hart voters prioritize veteran production as finalists loom post-first-round playoffs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 41°C or higher at 70.5% implied probability for Karachi's highest temperature on May 4, driven by the Pakistan Meteorological Department's (PMD) ongoing heatwave alert through May 6 amid persistent high-pressure systems suppressing cloud cover and fostering extreme pre-monsoon heating. Recent observational data shows Sindh temperatures climbing, with Karachi's "feels-like" values hitting 45°C in recent days, while AccuWeather models project May highs up to 42°C under clear skies and light winds. Historical May averages hover around 35°C but routinely exceed 40°C during such events, with urban heat island effects amplifying peaks at official PMD stations. NDMA warnings extend intense heat until May 5, though isolated thunderstorms northwest of the city could temper peaks; final resolution hinges on PMD's daily maximum reading amid model uncertainty.

markets.question: Will Nationalist Party win the third-most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, marking the third consecutive pause amid a rebound in inflation to 3.3% year-over-year in March CPI data—up sharply from 2.4% prior—and a resilient labor market with unemployment at 4.3% and nonfarm payrolls adding 178,000 jobs. The March dot plot projects one quarter-point cut in 2026 and another in 2027, targeting a low-3% range by year-end, though dissents signal internal debate on easing pace. Trader consensus in prediction markets reflects caution on near-term cuts, pricing in persistent inflation pressures and geopolitical risks. Key catalysts ahead include April CPI (May 10), nonfarm payrolls (May 8), and the June FOMC meeting.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart dated May 2, securing her as the ninth artist to top the chart in 2026 and fueling trader optimism for breakout momentum amid strong streaming and sales debut. This follows Ella Langley's dominant seven-week run with "Choosin' Texas," now at No. 2, while earlier frontrunners like Taylor Swift (two No. 1s), Bruno Mars, Bad Bunny, BTS, Harry Styles, and Mariah Carey (holiday re-entry) have locked in achievements. Current top 10 climbers, including Olivia Dean's "Man I Need" at No. 4 and Justin Bieber's stream-gaining "Daisies" at No. 8, signal competitive dynamics driven by radio airplay and viral TikTok traction. With weekly chart updates through December, upcoming summer releases and album campaigns from heavyweights like Drake and Ariana Grande represent key catalysts for shifting trader consensus on remaining contenders.

markets.question: Will "The Devil Wears Prada 2" score at least 85 on the Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the displayed Rotten Tomatoes “All Critics” Tomatometer score for The Devil Wears Prada 2 (2026) is at least equal to the specified number at 10:00 AM ET on May 4, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If, for any reason, the resolution data is unavailable at this market's specified end time, the resolution source will be checked until the relevant data is available. This market will resolve to “No” if no data is available by May 8, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following its May 1 wide theatrical release by 20th Century Studios, The Devil Wears Prada 2 has earned a 77% Tomatometer score on Rotten Tomatoes from 224 critic reviews, surpassing the original 2006 film's 75% and achieving Certified Fresh status in early tallies before slight adjustments. Traders note strong praise for Meryl Streep's iconic Miranda Priestly, Anne Hathaway's matured Andy Sachs, Emily Blunt's scene-stealing Emily, and Stanley Tucci's heartfelt Nigel, alongside timely satire on media consolidation and digital disruption in fashion publishing. Criticisms center on familiar beats lacking the debut's razor-sharp bite and occasional momentum lulls. With an 88% audience score, A- CinemaScore, and robust opening weekend tracking, the score may shift as more reviews pour in over the next few days, a common post-premiere dynamic for review aggregators.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Kostyantynivka by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Kostyantynivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.528896° N, 37.702514° E) by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Kostyantynivka will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the resolution date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of the specified area, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/p9N3w6yRVfjx6cuZ8

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces reached the outskirts of Kostiantynivka in Donetsk Oblast on May 2, launching persistent infiltration assaults with small infantry groups, as Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi reported ongoing repels and counter-sabotage measures. This escalation follows 83 attacks since late April, with Russia claiming nearby Novodmytrivka but failing to seize the town, per DeepState maps showing positions 1 km from southern edges amid contested grey zones. Ukrainian defenses hold the vital fortress belt hub—shielding Sloviansk-Kramatorsk—via drone strikes and attritional warfare, incurring high Russian casualties without confirmed urban advances. Traders weigh sustained pressure against defensive resilience and potential reinforcements ahead of summer offensives.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democratic victory at 92.5¢ in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District House race, reflecting incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

eventMetadata.context_description: The open-seat race in New York's 21st Congressional District, rated Solid Republican with an R+10 partisan lean where Donald Trump carried 60% in 2024, drives trader consensus favoring the Republican Party at 66.5% implied probability. Incumbent Elise Stefanik's departure for a gubernatorial bid opened the field, but a competitive GOP primary pits self-funded Anthony Constantino—backed by President Trump's April 21 endorsement and leading early polls—with state GOP-endorsed Assemblyman Robert Smullen, both boasting multimillion-dollar war chests. Democrats face a fragmented primary led by well-funded Blake Gendebien amid lower overall resources, underscoring the uphill battle in this battleground's northern New York terrain ahead of June 23 primaries and the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will the NYT front-page headlines say "AI" or "Intelligence" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed term is included in a headline on the New York Times front page between May 4 and May 10, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A headline is defined as the bolded or enlarged text directly preceding each article, previewing the article’s content and typically separated from the article’s text by a black line and byline. The primary headline for each story is the headline for that story with the largest text, typically appearing in bold font and above any other headlines or text for that article.

Sub-headlines, defined as additional bolded or enlarged text not separated from the primary headline by any text, will count, whether they appear before the byline or are partially surrounded by the article text but still adjacent to the primary headline. Pull quotes, however, or any bolded text not adjacent to the primary headline, will not count.

Banner headlines, defined as front-page headlines bordered on the sides only by white space, will count.

Image captions, article text, or any other text that does not constitute a headline, will not qualify.

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variations in capitalization, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will not count.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all slang forms, will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

If the listed term appears as part of a compound word, usage of that compound word qualifies, provided the listed term remains a distinct component of the compound. This does not include suffixes, prefixes, alternative tenses, or grammatical variations that alter the root word. (E.g. if the listed term is joy, killjoy qualifies but joyful does not. E.g. if the listed term is sun, sunflower qualifies but sunny does not.)
If the listed term is part of a hyphenated compound, usage of that hyphenated compound qualifies. (E.g. if the listed term is NATO, pro-NATO and anti-NATO qualify.)

The resolution source for this market will be the New York Times daily newspaper, including images of the front page posted daily at: https://nytimes.pressreader.com/the-new-york-times/

markets.question: Universitatea Craiova CS vs. Dinamo 1948: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Romania SuperLiga game between Universitatea Craiova CS and Dinamo 1948, scheduled for May 3 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Universitatea Craiova CS and Dinamo 1948 combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on lpf.ro. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Universitatea Craiova CS vs. Dinamo 1948 - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Romania SuperLiga game, scheduled for May 3 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: CS Universitatea Craiova, atop the SuperLiga championship group standings, holds strong home advantage at Stadionul Ion Oblemenco against fifth-placed Dinamo București 1948, with recent form favoring the hosts after a 1-0 away win over Rapid București on April 19. Dinamo notched a 2-1 home victory versus Universitatea Cluj last weekend but struggles historically, winning just 4 of 29 head-to-heads against Craiova's 15 triumphs and 10 draws—including a 1-0 Craiova win at Dinamo on March 19. Tomorrow's Romanian Cup quarterfinal at Dinamo could prompt rotations or fatigue risks; Craiova lacks backup goalkeeper Pavlo Isenko (meniscus tear) and winger Alexandru Iamandache (knee injury).

Istra enters off a 2-1 home win over Vukovar on April 18 and a tough Dinamo Zagreb fixture on April 22, showing decent home form, while Slaven drew 2-2 at Hajduk Split on April 17 before facing Lokomotiva on April 21, reflecting solid away resilience. Head-to-head records are balanced with frequent draws; Istra's Israel Isaac Ayuma remains sidelined by injury, but no other major absences reported in the past 30 days. Late-season motivation and home advantage could sway this evenly matched encounter.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Elon+Bull+Run+Parlay.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 88% implied probability against the "Elon Bull Run Parlay" resolving Yes, reflecting the multiplicative improbability of all three 2026 conditions materializing: Elon Musk achieving trillionaire status, announcing another child, and SpaceX executing 9+ Starship launches reaching space. Recent Tesla Q1 earnings on April 22 beat expectations with 17% profit growth, surging stock 5% and lifting Musk's net worth to around $800 billion—bolstering the trillionaire leg (73% in related market)—while Starship Flight 12 achieved orbit on April 7, prepping Flight 13. However, Starship cadence markets favor under 5 successes at 66%, no new baby announcements have surfaced, and trillionaire hurdles persist amid volatile tech valuations. Watch Flight 13 and SpaceX IPO progress for potential shifts.
